characters.name: Aaron Burton
characters.description: Aaron BurtonBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationSiblingsAlex Burton(brother)Series InformationSeasons2Count1Only Appearance"11:00 A.M."Portrayed byLukas Jann Aaron Burtonis the older brother ofAlex Burton. He is portrayed by Lukas Jann. Alex tells Langdon andPerlahthat his brother, Aaron, burned him with dry ice. When Perlah asks if Aaron dislikes him, Alex explains that he had asked Aaron to do it, intending for him to brand him in the same way he had previously done to Aaron.

characters.name: Abby Langdon
characters.description: Abby LangdonBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationMaritalFrank Langdon(husband)ChildrenTanner Langdon(son)Penny Langdon(daughter) Abby Langdonis the wife ofDr. Frank Langdonand the mother of their two children, includingTanner Langdon. Langdon shows Dana a picture of a puppy he recently got for his kids. Dana says it is cute but hopes it was not his idea. She explains that Abby does not need anything else to take care of, but Langdon argues that Abby loves dogs. He mentions that Tanner promised to take care of it, but Dana thinks the responsibility will ultimately fall on Abby.("8:00 A.M.")

characters.name: Agent Correa
characters.description: ICE Agent CorreaBasic InformationStatusAliveProfessional InformationOccupationFederal agentWorkplaceUnited States Immigration and Customs EnforcementSeries InformationSeasons2Count1Only Appearance"5:00 P.M."Portrayed byJosell MarianoAgent Correais an ICE agent that brought inPranita Shah. He is portrayed by Josell Mariano. Correa tellsRobbythatPranitatook a nasty fall and injured her shoulder. He reports that she screamed in pain when he andRussoput the restraints on her and says that he wants her evaluated before they process her. Correa remains with Pranita to keep an eye on her. Later, when Russo announces that Pranita has been cleared, Correa escorts her out while Russo arrestsJesseas well. The agents take both Pranita and Jesse to an undisclosed detention center.("5:00 P.M.")

characters.name: Agent Correa
characters.description: ICE Agent CorreaBasic InformationStatusAliveProfessional InformationOccupationFederal agentWorkplaceUnited States Immigration and Customs EnforcementSeries InformationSeasons2Count1Only Appearance"5:00 P.M."Portrayed byJosell MarianoAgent Correais an ICE agent that brought inPranita Shah. The agents take both Pranita and Jesse to an undisclosed detention center.("5:00 P.M.")

characters.name: Agent Russo
characters.description: ICE Agent RussoBasic InformationStatusAliveProfessional InformationOccupationFederal agentWorkplaceUnited States Immigration and Customs EnforcementSeries InformationSeasons2Count2First AppearanceLast Appearance"5:00 P.M.""6:00 P.M."Portrayed byJuju AlexanderAgent Russois an ICE agent that brought inPranita Shah. He is portrayed byJuju Alexander. Agents Russo andCorreabring their detainee, Pranita Shah, to the hospital. Russo explains that they were conducting a sweep at her restaurant when everyone in the kitchen fled, and Pranita was shoved down a flight of alley stairs. During her examination,Javadiperforms a drop arm test and determines that it is positive. Russo asks what that means, andRobbyexplains that Pranita will need an X-ray, though it is most likely a rotator cuff tear. Russo asks how long that will take, and Robby explains that everything is moving slower than usual because of the system shutdown, but they will try to fast-track her care.McKayoffers to call someone for Pranita, but Russo firmly refuses and says that she is not allowed to make any phone calls. He then asks for directions to the bathroom. While Russo stands guard, Pranita tells Javadi and McKay that she just wants to let her daughter know that she is okay. McKay grows frustrated when Russo continues refusing to let her make a phone call, but he insists that she cannot contact anyone until she has been processed.

He then asks for directions to the bathroom. While Russo stands guard, Pranita tells Javadi and McKay that she just wants to let her daughter know that she is okay. McKay grows frustrated when Russo continues refusing to let her make a phone call, but he insists that she cannot contact anyone until she has been processed.

characters.name: Ahmad Zidan
characters.description: Ahmad ZidanBasic InformationStatusAliveProfessional InformationOccupationSecurity guardWorkplacePittsburgh Trauma Medical CenterSeries InformationSeasons1,2Count18First AppearanceLast Appearance"10:00 A.M."OngoingPortrayed byJohnath DavisAhmad Zidanis a security guard atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. He is portrayed byJohnath Davis. Ahmad asks about the commotion out in the ambulance bay. Ziggler, surprised, says she thought Ahmad was watching the monitors and questions how he could have missed it. Ahmad says he saw the ambulance pull out but assumed she was in it. He asks why Ziggler left the keys in the ignition. Ziggler says it's protocol, but both Ahmad and Kerns disagree, insisting that it is not. Ziggler admits he didn't call the cops because two of them are already sitting in the cafeteria having breakfast. He starts a bet on the stolen ambulance chase, putting ten dollars on the guess that they will not make it to the county line.("10:00 A.M.") He tells Princess and Donnie that the ambulance chase is on live TV. He and Princess both guess that the over-under on clipped cars should be fifteen. He watches with Princess in the waiting room as the chase loops back around.("11:00 A.M.")

characters.name: Alana Dunn
characters.description: Alana DunnBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationParentsSilas Dunn(father)Susan Dunn(mother)Series InformationSeasons1Count2First AppearanceLast Appearance"12:00 P.M.""1:00 P.M."Portrayed byIvy George Alana Dunnis the daughter ofSilas Dunn. She was a victim of child molestation by the hands of her father. She is portrayed byIvy George. Alana arrives at the ED with her mother and gets an update from Santos. Santos tells them that her father's condition is serious, but he's stable for now. He offers to take them to see him, which Alana wants to do. However, Susan shuts it down, insisting they'll wait in the waiting room. Alana tries to argue, but Susan begs her to let the doctors do what they need to do for her dad.("12:00 P.M.")

characters.name: Alex
characters.description: AlexPatient InformationDiagnosisAbdominal GSWNurse(s)Jesse Van HornPerlah AlawiDoctor(s)Melissa KingYolanda GarciaMichael RobinavitchFred MillerSeries InformationSeasons1Count1Only Appearance"8:00 A.M."Portrayed ByBobby Ray Cauley Jr. Alexis a patient atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. He is portrayed by Bobby Ray Cauley Jr. Mel steps out to the ambulance bay to calm herself down by singing "Savage" by Megan Thee Stallion. She sees Alex climbing out of a car, claiming he needs help and that he has been shot. Mel calls for the medics and quickly assesses him. He says it hurts. The medics bring him inside, and Mel assures him they are going to take good care of him. She informs the team that she has an abdominal gunshot wound in the ambulance bay and calls a code trauma.

characters.description: Allen BillingsPatient InformationDiagnosisLuxatio erectaStatusAliveTreatmentReductionSurgeryNurse(s)Princess Dela CruzJordan PrescottDoctor(s)Michael RobinavitchBaran Al-HashimiYolanda GarciaCassie McKayMelissa KingSeries InformationSeasons2Count3First AppearanceLast Appearance"7:00 A.M.""11:00 A.M."Portrayed ByMichael Cognata Allen Billingsis a patient atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. characters.name: Amanda Jones
characters.description: Amanda JonesBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationMaritalDrew Jones(husband)SiblingsDanny (brother)ChildrenTyler Jones(son)Series InformationSeasons1Count2First AppearanceLast Appearance"7:00 A.M.""8:00 A.M."Portrayed byKim Bonifay Amanda Jonesis the mother ofTyler Jones, the 4-year-old boy who accidentally ate a weed gummy. She is portrayed byKim Bonifay. Amanda and Drew are concerned about their son Tyler, who is unusually lethargic. Amanda notes that he barely flinched during the blood test, even though he is usually quite active. Their entire house is childproof, so she wonders what could be wrong with him.Langdonfinds something gelatinous in Tyler's mouth and suspects he may have gotten into bath beads or laundry pods. Amanda insists they don't keep anything like that in the house. Langdon then asks about gummies, and Drew admits he had some pot gummies in his coat pocket, given to him by Amanda's brother. When Amanda finds out, she becomes furious and tells Drew to leave.("7:00 A.M.")

characters.name: Amaya
characters.description: AmayaBiographical InformationAge32Familial InformationMaritalUnnamed fiancéPatient InformationDiagnosisPolycystic ovary syndromeIntermittent ovary torsionStatusAliveTreatmentSurgeryNurse(s)Jesse Van HornDoctor(s)Cassie McKayDennis WhitakerSeries InformationSeasons2Count1Only Appearance"3:00 P.M."Portrayed ByCharrell Mack Amayais a patient atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. She is portrayed by Charrell Mack. McKay examines Amaya, who is sweating heavily and clearly in pain. Amaya explains that she usually manages the pain with Aleve, but this episode has been much worse than usual. She says it feels like the same type of pain she has experienced before from her PCOS. McKay orders a urinalysis and a urine pregnancy test and gives her a shot of Toradol for the pain. Amaya insists that she is not pregnant, but McKay explains that the test is simply hospital policy. As McKay writes notes on paper, Amaya asks why they are still doing that. McKay tells her it is only for the day.

characters.name: Amber Phillips
characters.description: Amber PhillipsBiographical InformationAge6DiedSeptember 5, 2025Familial InformationSiblingsBella Phillips(sister)ParentsGina Phillips(mother)Ryan Phillips(father)OthersFrances Phillips(grandmother)Patient InformationDiagnosisAccidental drowningStatusDeceasedTreatmentCompressionsWarm IV fluidsNurse(s)Mateo DiazSophie AugetTim SalingerDoctor(s)Michael RobinavitchHeather CollinsDennis WhitakerMelissa KingSeries InformationSeasons1Count1Only Appearance"2:00 P.M."Portrayed ByHadley Smith Amber Phillipswas a patient atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. She drowned while saving her sister. She is portrayed by Hadley Smith. Amber is brought into the emergency department by EMTs after being found at the bottom of a home pool with an unknown downtime. She is in asystole on the monitor. She has been intubated with a cuffed 4.5 tube and has a 22-gauge IV in the left antecubital. She received 0.25 milligrams of epinephrine three minutes ago. Her rectal temperature is only 85 degrees, but it needs to reach at least 90 degrees for the doctors to have any chance of restarting her heart. Collins orders 250 cc of heated saline and continuous core temperature monitoring.

She is in asystole on the monitor. She has been intubated with a cuffed 4.5 tube and has a 22-gauge IV in the left antecubital. She received 0.25 milligrams of epinephrine three minutes ago. Her rectal temperature is only 85 degrees, but it needs to reach at least 90 degrees for the doctors to have any chance of restarting her heart. Collins orders 250 cc of heated saline and continuous core temperature monitoring.

characters.name: Amy Joon
characters.description: Amy JoonBiographical InformationStatusAliveTitle(s)M.D.Professional InformationOccupationDermatologistDepartmentDermatologyWorkplacePittsburgh Trauma Medical CenterSeries InformationSeasons2Count1Only Appearance"1:00 P.M."Portrayed byCeleste DenDr. Amy Joonis the Chief of Dermatology atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. She is portrayed by Celeste Den. Raymond Javadibrings Amy down to the emergency department to visit hisdaughterduring her shift. He introduces Amy as the smartest person he knows and the Chief of Dermatology. He mentions that Javadi's very first shift in the ER happened on the day of the PittFest shooting. Amy says she has heard incredible stories about that day, including tales of Javadi's impressive work as a medical student. Javadi brushes off the praise, insisting that she was just trying to stay out of the way. Amy then receives a phone call and answers it. She explains that she is doing a favor for someone at the mayor's office who needs Mohs surgery for a basal cell carcinoma and adds that she is also grilling for thirty people later. Before leaving, she wishes them both a happy Fourth of July and invites Javadi to grab coffee sometime.("1:00 P.M.")

characters.name: Amy Miller
characters.description: Amy MillerBiographical InformationAge28Familial InformationMaritalTeddy Miller† (husband)ChildrenTheo Miller (son)Series InformationSeasons1,2Count2First Appearance"4:00 P.M."Portrayed byBailey Gavulic Amy MillerisTeddy Miller's widow. She is portrayed byBailey Gavulic. Amy arrives at the emergency department just before Teddy is intubated. Robby explains that they need to intubate before his throat swells and it becomes difficult for him to breathe. He tells them that if there is anything they want to say to each other, now is the time. Teddy reassures Amy that he is okay. She asks if she can kiss him and they kiss. She tells him that he is right there.

characters.name: Ana Diaz
characters.description: Ana DiazBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationSiblings2 unnamed brothersParentsOrlando Diaz(father)Lorrie Diaz(mother)Series InformationSeasonsSeason 2Count3First AppearanceLast Appearance"9:00 A.M.""11:00 A.M."Portrayed bySavannah Ruiz Ana Diazis the daughter ofOrlando Diaz. She works at a cafe to help her dual-income parents financially. She is portrayed bySavannah Ruiz. Samira tells Ana that Orlando is improving after receiving two liters of fluid and that his blood sugar has dropped from 521 to 436. Ana asks if that is good news, and Samira reassures her that they are moving in the right direction. She asks if Ana was able to reach her mother. Ana explains that Lorrie is on her way, but since she does not have a car and relies on the bus, it takes longer for her to get to the hospital.("9:00 A.M.")

characters.name: Antoine Dubois
characters.description: Antoine DuboisProfessional InformationOccupationMedical assistantDepartmentEmergencyWorkplacePittsburgh Trauma Medical CenterSeries InformationSeasons1,2Count12First Appearance"10:00 A.M."Portrayed byJermaine WilliamsAntoine Duboisis a medical assistant working in the emergency department atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. He is portrayed byJermaine Williams. Antoine gets a new motorcycle and is eager to show it off to his friends. He joins in on the stolen ambulance bet, wanting to put in $8, butAhmadtells him bets have to be in multiples of five.Danawalks in and breaks up the gambling circle, reminding Antoine that he is supposed to be in triage. She tells him that Dennison needs a knee immobilizer and crutches.("10:00 A.M.") When the ambulance thieves are brought into the ED, Antoine claims he knew they were gonna crash. Paolo wonders why he didn't bet on the crash, but Antoine says he doesn't want anyone to get hurt—he thinks it's bad karma. Ahmad tells them that Collins hit the full parlay: in their zone, crash, and frat boys. Larry complains that the rich just get richer, and Antoine tells him he needs to practice more gratitude. Larry says he'd be a lot more grateful if his paycheck were bigger, and Antoine agrees.("12:00 P.M.")

Paolo wonders why he didn't bet on the crash, but Antoine says he doesn't want anyone to get hurt—he thinks it's bad karma. Ahmad tells them that Collins hit the full parlay: in their zone, crash, and frat boys. Larry complains that the rich just get richer, and Antoine tells him he needs to practice more gratitude. Larry says he'd be a lot more grateful if his paycheck were bigger, and Antoine agrees.("12:00 P.M.")

characters.name: Arun Mehta
characters.description: Arun MehtaBiographical InformationStatusAliveTitle(s)M.D.Professional InformationOccupationNeurologistDepartmentNeurologyWorkplacePittsburgh Trauma Medical CenterSeries InformationSeasons1Count1Only Appearance"4:00 P.M."Portrayed byKinner Shah "What do you call a Tyrannosaurus under stress? [...] A nervous Rex."― Dr. Mehta Dr. Arun Mehtais a stroke neurologist atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. He is portrayed by Kinner Shah. Part of Dr. Mehta's medical philosophy is that laughter is the best medicine. He tells jokes and makes puns about medicine with his colleagues and patients. According toSamira Mohan, Dr. Mehta thinks that "if a joke helps raise spirits and makes people feel better, then he's all for it."[1]
characters.personality_section: Part of Dr. Mehta's medical philosophy is that laughter is the best medicine. He tells jokes and makes puns about medicine with his colleagues and patients. According toSamira Mohan, Dr. Mehta thinks that "if a joke helps raise spirits and makes people feel better, then he's all for it."[1]

[...] A nervous Rex."― Dr. Mehta Dr. Arun Mehtais a stroke neurologist atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. He is portrayed by Kinner Shah. Part of Dr. Mehta's medical philosophy is that laughter is the best medicine. He tells jokes and makes puns about medicine with his colleagues and patients. According toSamira Mohan, Dr. Mehta thinks that "if a joke helps raise spirits and makes people feel better, then he's all for it."[1]

characters.name: Baby Jane Doe
characters.description: Baby Jane DoeBiographical InformationAliasBaby Jane Doe (by everyone)Superbaby (by Robby)Little Miss Sunshine (by Santos)Patient InformationDiagnosisRhinovirusStatusAliveTreatmentCeftriaxoneTylenolNurse(s)Donnie DonahueJesse Van HornDana EvansTim SalingerDoctor(s)Baran Al-HashimiMichael RobinavitchSamira MohanDesmond WolkeNazely ToomarianSeries InformationSeasons2Count8First AppearanceLast Appearance"7:00 A.M.""9:00 P.M."Portrayed ByLuna & Luca Pinder et al. "Baby Jane Doe"is a patient atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. She is portrayed by 14 baby actors, including Luna and Luca Pinder.[1][2] Phyliciadiscovers an abandoned infant in the women's restroom and immediately alertsDonnie, who rushes in to get the baby.Lupethen makes an announcement over the intercom asking if anyone is missing a baby or left a child in the restroom.LangdonasksOlsenwhether he remembers seeing a woman enter the hospital with a baby but leave without one. Olsen says he does not recall but agrees to review the security footage.

"Baby Jane Doe"is a patient atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. She is portrayed by 14 baby actors, including Luna and Luca Pinder.[1][2] Phyliciadiscovers an abandoned infant in the women's restroom and immediately alertsDonnie, who rushes in to get the baby.Lupethen makes an announcement over the intercom asking if anyone is missing a baby or left a child in the restroom.LangdonasksOlsenwhether he remembers seeing a woman enter the hospital with a baby but leave without one. Olsen says he does not recall but agrees to review the security footage.

characters.name: Baran Al-Hashimi
characters.description: Baran Al-HashimiBiographical InformationAliasDr. Al (by everyone)Dr. Wishful Thinking (by Garcia)Robo-Doc (by Santos)Age40StatusAliveTitle(s)M.D.Familial InformationMaritalUnnamed ex-husbandChildrenUnnamed sonProfessional InformationOccupationAttending PhysicianSpecialityEmergency MedicineDepartmentEmergencyWorkplacePittsburgh Trauma Medical CenterEducation & BackgroundAlma MaterStanford University[1]Series InformationSeasons2Count15First AppearanceLast Appearance"7:00 A.M.""9:00 P.M"Portrayed bySepideh Moafi "Everything I've done in my career is an effort to improve the system. Just because you know it's broken doesn't mean you stop trying."― Al-Hashimi[source] Dr. Baran Al-Hashimiis an attending who arrives atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Centerfrom the VA Hospital, where she previously worked with Dr.Mel Kingand Dr.Samira Mohan. She is portrayed bySepideh Moafi. Dr. Al-Hashimi is shown to be determined, analytical and focused towards fixing the broken system of healthcare. Despite being new, she is confident enough to implement new ideas during her temporary stay, such as the use of patient passports and artificial intelligence to cut down on time spent on charting. Unlike Dr. Robby, Dr. Al-Hashimi is far more composed and restrained emotionally, whereas the former, in contrast, is impulsive and emotional. She harbors a heightened sense of justice and is a strong patient advocate, as seen when she attempts to get Gus better care after he's recommended to return to prison despite being malnourished. Beneath her confidence in her abilities and choices, however, hides a degree of stubbornness, which comes into play when she discovers the return of her absence seizures during her first day as attending and attempts to hide it from administration despite knowing it could prove dangerous for her in treating patients and driving.

characters.name: Barrett Dunkle
characters.description: Barrett DunkleBiographical InformationAge34Patient InformationDiagnosisPneumothoraxStatusAliveTreatmentSurgeryNurse(s)Tim SalingerJamie WatkinsDoctor(s)Jack AbbotCrus HendersonMichael RobinavitchSeries InformationSeasons2Count1Only Appearance"8:00 P.M."Portrayed ByJoshua Dov Barrett Dunkleis a patient atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. He is portrayed by Joshua Dov. Barrett is brought in after being stabbed in the chest with an American flag during a bar fight. Despite the injury, his vital signs are relatively stable; he is saturating 91% on 15 liters, with a blood pressure of 132/87 and a heart rate of 112. When he sees his opponent,Toby, arrive next to him, Barrett becomes agitated and declares that he is going to pull the flag out of his chest and stick it him instead. He insists that Toby cheated and continues yelling in pain as he is transported to the trauma room. Abbot tells him they will administer additional pain medication but instructs him to calm down first.

characters.name: Becca King
characters.description: Becca KingBiographical InformationStatusAliveAge27Familial InformationSiblingsMelissa King(twin sister)ParentsUnnamed mother †Series InformationSeasons1,2Count7First Appearance"12:00 P.M."Portrayed byTal Anderson Rebecca "Becca" King[1]is the twin sister ofDr. Mel King, who is autistic and lives in a care facility. She is portrayed byTal Anderson. Becca has autism with special needs. After her mother died, her sister Mel became her primary caregiver.[2]

It's me, Bella. Thank you for saving me. When you come home, I promise I won't touch your toys without asking. And I'll try not to fight, because you're my best friend in the whole entire world. I love you."― Bella Bella PhillipsisAmber Phillips' sister. She is portrayed by Olivia Fokova.

characters.name: Ben Baker
characters.description: Ben BakerBiographical InformationAge8BornNovember 21, 2017Patient InformationDiagnosisLaryngeal fractureStatusAliveTreatmentSlash tracheostomySurgeryNurse(s)Perlah AlawiJesse Van HornTim SalingerJordan PrescottDoctor(s)Michael RobinavitchBaran Al-HashimiFrank LangdonDennis WhitakerYolanda GarciaSeries InformationSeasons2Count1Only Appearance"4:00 P.M."Portrayed ByBronson Arrivillaga Benjamin "Ben" Baker[1]is a patient atPittsburgh Trauma Medical Center. He is portrayed by Bronson Arrivillaga. Ben is life-flighted to the hospital after being thrown off a waterslide, falling six or seven feet, and landing in a tree. He arrives with severe neck trauma. The flight nurse reports that they were unable to intubate him in the field. Ben's oxygen saturation is in the 80s, his blood pressure is 85 over 60, and his pulse is 50. Al-Hashimi suspects a possible laryngeal fracture. Whitaker points out that Ben is hypoxic and bradycardic, and Robby warns that they need to establish an airway before he goes into cardiac arrest. Ben does not respond when Langdon calls to him and shows no purposeful movement to pain. Whitaker asks if they should perform a cricothyrotomy, but Robby says Ben is too young for that. Whitaker then suggests a needle crike, but Robby explains they would not be able to ventilate through it. Ben's heart rate continues to drop as he approaches cardiac arrest.
